Mutual Visa Exemption Agreement for all valid passport holders signed 23 October 2021, in force January 2022. Listed as visa-free (NO) on official immigration.gov.bz visa-who-qualify page as of June 2026 re-fetch; the page shows NO duration/day-limit number for Russia (the duration column is blank). A prior re-verification claimed a 30-day cap on the page; that was NOT supported by the live page and has been reverted to null. Treaty text states 90 days in any 180-day period; travellers should verify operative limit at border.

visa_policy.notes: Official Belize immigration site lists visa-free nationalities at immigration.gov.bz/visa/visa-who-qualify/. Standard visitor entry allows up to 30 days; some nationalities have specific limits (30, 90, or 180 days) as noted. No e-visa or ETA system found on official pages. Venezuela, Ecuador, and Dominican Republic had visa-free privileges rescinded for regular passports (news reports 2022); diplomatic/official passports remain exempt. Russia is now listed on the official page following the Mutual Visa Exemption Agreement ratified 2022 (all passport types, 90 days). Benin ordinary passports covered under November 2024 ratified treaty (not yet reflected on immigration.gov.bz listing). Tunisia and Turkey confirmed present on live official page during June 2026 re-fetch.

conditional_access.conditions: Must hold a valid multiple-entry visa issued by the United States of America. Stay not exceeding 30 days.
Must hold a valid US Permanent Residence Card. Stay not exceeding 30 days.
Must hold a valid multiple-entry Schengen visa. Stay not exceeding 30 days.
Must hold a valid multiple-entry visa issued by Canada. Stay not exceeding 30 days.
Must hold a valid Canadian Permanent Residence Card. Stay not exceeding 30 days.

Holders of diplomatic or official passports issued by Pakistan are exempt from visa requirements to enter, exit, transit and stay in Belize for a period not exceeding 90 days, whether in one or multiple periods from the date of first entry. Agreement signed 18 September 2024; Senate ratification motion passed November 2024. Does not apply to ordinary passport holders.

Holders of diplomatic, service or official passports issued by Hungary are permitted to enter, exit and transit through Belize without a visa for a period not exceeding 90 days in any 180-day period. Agreement signed 28 May 2021 in Mexico City; Senate ratification motion passed September 2021. Ordinary passport holders are also visa-free under Belize's standard policy.
Only holders of diplomatic and official passports are exempt from visa requirement. Regular/ordinary passport holders require a visa since January 2022 rescission.
Only holders of diplomatic and official passports are exempt from visa requirement. Regular/ordinary passport holders require a visa since January 2022 rescission.
Only holders of diplomatic and official passports are exempt from visa requirement. Regular/ordinary passport holders require a visa since January 2022 rescission.

Agreement Between Belize and the Benelux States (Belgium, Netherlands, Luxembourg) on the Exemption from Visa Requirements for Holders of Valid Diplomatic Passports. Ratified by Belize Senate May 2025. Covers diplomatic passports only; does not extend to service, official, or ordinary passport holders. Stay limit not specified in published Senate motion.

cbi.notes: Belize previously operated an Economic Citizenship by Investment Programme. The Belizean Nationality Amendment Act 2014 repealed the provision enabling economic citizenship. The Economic Citizenship (Abolition of Rights) Act 2023 (Act No. 54 of 2023) further abolished remaining rights, blocking any person from relying on an economic citizenship relationship to obtain Belizean nationality - including descendants of former economic citizens. As of 2023, no CBI program exists in Belize.

programs.name: Temporary Residence by Investment
programs.notes: Available to persons who have made or are making a substantial commercial investment in Belize. Requires evidence of investments valued at not less than BZ$500,000. Supporting documents include real estate title, bank statements, company registration, trade license. Duration and fees not specified on official page. Renewable annually. No explicit path to permanent residence or citizenship stated on official page.

programs.name: Qualified Retired Persons (QRP) Incentive Programme
programs.notes: Administered by the Belize Tourism Board. For persons aged 40+ with minimum annual income of USD $24,000 (USD $2,000/month) from sources outside Belize. Eligible income: pensions, annuities, Social Security, investments, inheritance, savings, retirement plans. Approved currencies: USD, GBP, EUR, CAD. Minimum 30 consecutive days annually in Belize. Application fees: US$150 program application + US$1,000 per applicant + US$750 per dependent + US$200 QRP ID card. Annual renewal: US$25. Grants Belize Residency Status (QRP Resident Card). Tax exemptions on foreign-sourced income. Option to operate a business with minimum BZ$1,000,000 investment employing 5 Belizean citizens. Duty and tax exemption on household effects, motor vehicles (max 5 years old), light aircraft, and motor boats in first year. Qualifying nationalities: Commonwealth nations (excluding Belize), USA, EU member states, select Asia-Pacific countries, all South American nations. Program confirmed active as of June 2026 re-verification.

programs.name: Work Where You Vacation Programme
programs.eligibility: Citizens or permanent residents of EU, UK, USA, or Canada who are employed outside Belize; minimum annual income of USD $75,000 for individuals or USD $100,000 for couples/families; clear criminal record check (no more than 6 months old); travel insurance with minimum coverage of US $50,000; notarized banking reference and account statement
programs.processing_time: A few weeks (no fixed timeline stated officially)
programs.notes: Allows remote workers to reside in Belize for up to 180 days. Waives work permit for participant and student permits for dependent children. Fee: BZD $500 per adult, BZD $200 per child under 18. Fee paid upon airport arrival. Apply via email to Belize Immigration Department: visas@immigration.gov.bz or secretary@immigration.gov.bz. Administered via Belize Tourism Board and Immigration Department partnership. Not a path to permanent residence. Program confirmed active as of June 2026 re-verification.

agreements: CARICOM (Caribbean Community) - full free movement under Enhanced Cooperation Protocol effective 1 October 2025, with Barbados, Dominica, and St. Vincent and the Grenadines; nationals may reside, work, and remain indefinitely
Belize-Russia Mutual Visa Exemption - all valid passport holders, up to 90 days in any 180-day period; agreement signed 23 October 2021, in force January 2022

Belize-Benin Visa Exemption - diplomatic, official, service, and ordinary passport holders; agreement ratified by Belize Senate November 2024; NOTE: as of June 2026 the official immigration.gov.bz page still lists Benin as visa-required; ordinary passport holders should verify before travel
Belize-Pakistan Visa Exemption - diplomatic and official passports only; agreement signed September 2024, ratified November 2024
Belize-Hungary Diplomatic/Service/Official Passport Exemption - 90 days; agreement signed May 2021, ratified September 2021
Belize-Benelux States (Belgium, Netherlands, Luxembourg) Diplomatic Passport Exemption - ratified by Belize Senate May 2025

data_quality.validation_notes: Re-verified June 2026 against live official sources. Previous corrections (pre-2026-06-27): (1) Added Russian Federation to visa_free - confirmed on live immigration.gov.bz page and via ratified 2022 treaty covering all passport types; (2) Added Tunisia and Turkey to visa_free; (3) Added La Deserada and Ross Dependency (territories) to visa_free; (4) Added Benin to visa_free based on November 2024 Senate-ratified treaty (ordinary passports); (5) Added Benelux diplomatic passport conditional_access. Corrections 2026-06-27 re-verification against live immigration.gov.bz/visa/visa-who-qualify/: (A) REMOVED Benin from visa_free - live official page explicitly lists Benin as 'YES' (visa required); despite Senate ratification of treaty the page remains as visa-required; per safety rules classified DOWN; moved to gaps. (B) CORRECTED Russian Federation max_stay_days from 90 to 30 - live official page states 30-day limit; prior record used treaty text (90 days) which diverges from what the page operatively enforces. (C) ADDED Belgium to visa_free as ordinary-passport visa-free - live page shows 'NO' for Belgium under ordinary passport; previously Belgium was only in conditional_access (Benelux diplomatic-only). (D) ADDED St. Kitts and Nevis to visa_free - present on live page; was entirely missing from record. (E) ADDED St. Martin (territory) to visa_free - present on live page; was entirely missing from record. Corrections 2026-06-29 re-verification against live immigration.gov.bz/visa/visa-who-qualify/: (F) ADDED Sovereign Base Areas of Akrotiri and Sovereign Base Area Dhekelia to visa_free - both listed on live page ('NO'); previously entirely missing from record. (G) Gabon confirmed as visa-REQUIRED on live page - not in visa_free list (correct). (H) Mauritius confirmed as visa-free (NO) on live page - existing record correct. (I) La Deserada confirmed present on live page (spelled 'La Desarada') - existing record correct. (J) Nauru shows ambiguous 'YES - NO' notation on live page - kept in visa_free with gap note; record preserved pending clarification. (K) Russian Federation max_stay_days confirmed as null on live page (no day limit shown) - consistent with current record. No e-visa, ETA, or VOA categories found.
gaps: No e-visa or ETA system found on official Belize immigration pages - confirmed absent as of June 2026 re-fetch.
Visa-on-arrival: no official documentation found for any VOA category; omitted.
Maximum stay durations for many visa-free nationalities not explicitly stated on official page (shown as null) - only specific countries have explicit day-limits listed.
Exact duration and fees for Temporary Residence by Investment permit not stated on official immigration.gov.bz page.
Path from Temporary Residence to Permanent Residence or citizenship not officially documented.
Jamaica visa restrictions (reportedly imposed March 2023 per news) not reflected in current official page listing - Jamaica remains on the official visa-free list; defer to official page.

Benin: live immigration.gov.bz page lists as 'YES' (visa required) as of June 2026 re-fetch despite Senate-ratified ordinary-passport exemption (November 2024); removed from visa_free and noted here pending update to official page. Ordinary passport holders should verify directly with Belize immigration before travel.

Russia: live page shows 30-day limit; treaty text states 90 days in any 180-day period. The operative cap applied at the border may differ from the page - travellers should verify. Record now reflects official page (30 days).
No OCI (India Overseas Citizen) cardholder exemption found on any official Belize government source - not recorded.
Full list of qualifying nationalities for QRP programme not enumerated on BTB official page.
Benelux (May 2025) agreement stay-limit not specified in available published Senate motion text.
Full historical catalogue of Belize bilateral diplomatic/official passport waiver agreements not available from a single official source; years 2022–2023 Senate archives not exhaustively checked.

Nauru: official Belize immigration page shows a conflicting 'YES - NO' entry for Nauru as of June 2026 re-fetch; this appears to be a data entry error on the official page. Nauru is retained in visa_free pending official clarification. If YES means visa required, this entry should be removed.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa (Single Entry)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals wishing to visit Belize for holiday, leisure, or exploration of settlement possibilities, who are not visa-exempt.
visa_types.notes: Belize visa fees are published in Belize Dollars (BZD). Single-entry visa for all nationalities (except China and specified Asian nationals): BZD $200 (~USD $100). For China nationals: BZD $3,000 (~USD $1,500). For India, Nepal, Thailand, Burma, Bangladesh, Pakistan, Sri Lanka nationals: BZD $750 (~USD $375). Valid for 6 months from issue; allows 30-day stay. Applications submitted at nearest Belizean diplomatic/consular mission or at Belmopan HQ for applicants already in Belize. US PR card, US multi-entry visa, Schengen multi-entry visa, or Canadian visa/PR card holders are exempt from needing a visa (max 30-day stay). Fees effective May 1, 2020.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa (Multiple Entry)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals requiring multiple entries into Belize for tourism or temporary business purposes within a 12-month period.
visa_types.notes: Multiple-entry visa valid for 12 months. Fees in BZD: all other nationalities BZD $300 (~USD $150); China nationals BZD $4,000 (~USD $2,000); India, Nepal, Thailand, Burma, Bangladesh, Pakistan, Sri Lanka nationals BZD $1,000 (~USD $500). Available to temporary permit holders and regular business/tourism visitors. Fees effective May 1, 2020.

visa_types.name: Visitor's Permit Extension
visa_types.purpose: Extends a visitor's legal stay in Belize by an additional 30 days beyond initial entry allowance.
visa_types.notes: Fee: BZD $200 (~USD $100) for all nationals. Grants an additional 30 days of legal stay. Cannot be used to engage in employment (paid or unpaid) or conduct business without Director of Immigration consent. Applicants requiring a visa must first obtain one before being granted a visitor's permit extension. Available at any Immigration Office in Belize.

visa_types.name: Employment Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals officially hired locally, conducting consultations, representing overseas companies, working as foreign media personnel, or performing technical work assignments in Belize.
visa_types.notes: Entry visa required prior to taking up employment. Separate Work Permit must be obtained after entry. Visa fee structure same as tourist visa (nationality-based: BZD $200 / $750 / $3,000 for single entry). Applicant must be residing in Belize and hold a valid Belize visa before a Work Permit can be issued.

visa_types.name: Temporary Employment Permit (Work Permit)
visa_types.purpose: Authorizes foreign nationals to engage in paid employment in Belize across professional, technical, agricultural, entertainment, or self-employed categories.
visa_types.notes: Fees in BZD vary by category: professional/technical workers BZD $3,000/year (~USD $1,500); general agricultural workers BZD $1,500/year (~USD $750); seasonal agricultural workers BZD $150/season; religious workers BZD $200/year; entertainers BZD $600–$1,500/week. Applicant must already be residing in Belize with valid visa. Process: employer submits to Labour Department; within 30 days applicant is notified; then visits Immigration Office for issuance. Categories include: professional (university degree), technical, general laborer, seasonal agricultural, self-employed, entertainer, religious/cultural volunteer, trader, peddler, domestic staff.

visa_types.name: Student Permit
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals enrolled at primary, secondary, or tertiary educational institutions in Belize.
visa_types.notes: Fees in BZD: all nationalities (except below) BZD $200 (~USD $100) per school year/semester; India, Nepal, Thailand, Burma, Bangladesh, Pakistan, Sri Lanka nationals BZD $750; China nationals BZD $3,000. Validity: one school year for primary/secondary students; one semester for tertiary students. Processed on submission - Student Permit sticker placed in passport at time of application. Requires institutional letter, financial support letter, two passport photos, birth certificate, parent/guardian authority letter, sponsor's bank statement, and return ticket (tertiary extra-regional students only). Fees effective May 1, 2020.

visa_types.name: Student Visa
visa_types.purpose: Entry visa for foreign nationals who require a visa to enter Belize and plan to study at a Belizean educational institution.
visa_types.notes: Entry visa to be obtained before traveling; upon arrival applicant applies for Student Permit. Visa fee same structure as tourist/employment visa (BZD $200 / $750 / $3,000 depending on nationality). Must be at primary, secondary, or tertiary level institution in Belize.

visa_types.name: Work Where You Vacation Programme (Long Stay Permit)
visa_types.purpose: Allows remote workers employed outside Belize to live and work remotely in Belize for up to 6 months per calendar year.
visa_types.notes: Fee: BZD $500 (~USD $250) per adult; BZD $200 (~USD $100) per child under 18. Paid upon airport arrival. Eligibility: citizens or permanent residents of EU, UK, USA, or Canada; employed outside Belize; minimum annual income USD $75,000 (individual) or USD $100,000 (with dependents); clear police record (within 6 months); travel insurance minimum USD $50,000 coverage; notarized banking reference and account statement. Work restrictions: no employment in Belize permitted. Not extendable - must not exceed 6 months in calendar year. Waives work permit requirement for participant and student permits for dependent children. Also administered via travelbelize.org as 'Work Where You Vacation Programme'. Apply via email to visas@immigration.gov.bz or secretary@immigration.gov.bz prior to travel. Confirmed active as of June 2026.

visa_types.name: Qualified Retired Persons (QRP) Programme
visa_types.purpose: Residency program for retirees aged 40+ with foreign income who wish to live in Belize on a long-term basis with special tax and import privileges.
visa_types.notes: Administered by the Belize Tourism Board (BTB). Minimum age: 45 (updated from 40 - verify with BTB). Minimum annual income: USD $24,000 (USD $2,000/month) from sources outside Belize. Eligible income: pensions, annuities, Social Security, investments, inheritance, savings, retirement plans. Application fees: USD $150 (program application) + USD $1,000 per applicant + USD $750 per dependent + USD $200 QRP ID card. Annual renewal: USD $25. Grants Belize Residency (QRP Resident Card). Minimum 30 consecutive days per year in Belize required. Duty/tax exemptions on household effects, vehicle (max 5 years old), light aircraft, and motor boats in first year. Tax exemptions on foreign-sourced income. Option to operate a business with minimum BZD $1,000,000 investment employing 5 Belizean citizens. Qualifying nationalities: Commonwealth nations (excluding Belize), USA, EU member states, select Asia-Pacific and South American nations. Confirmed active as of June 2026.

visa_types.name: Temporary Residence by Investment
visa_types.purpose: Grants temporary residence status to foreign nationals who have made or are making substantial commercial investment in Belize.
visa_types.notes: Minimum investment threshold: BZD $500,000 in commercial investment. Required documentation includes: real estate title or lease agreement, bank statements, company registration documents, trade license. Renewable annually. No explicit path to permanent residence or citizenship stated on official page. Duration and specific fees not published on official page - verify at Belmopan Immigration Headquarters.

visa_types.name: Dependent Permit
visa_types.purpose: For immediate family members (spouse, child, parent, or sibling) of Belize residents who are of retired age (55+), disabled, or children under 5 years of age.
visa_types.notes: Fees in BZD: all nationalities (except below) BZD $200 (~USD $100); India, Nepal, Thailand, Burma, Bangladesh, Pakistan, Sri Lanka nationals BZD $750; China nationals BZD $3,000. Applicant must be sponsored by a Belize resident who can provide housing. Qualifying dependents: persons of retired age (55+), disabled persons, or children under 5. Required documents: financial support letter, photographs, birth/marriage certificate, passport, proof of Belizean identity of sponsor, bank statement, 1-year return ticket. Processed on submission with permit sticker placed in passport. Fees effective May 1, 2020.

visa_types.name: Diplomatic/Official Visa Exemption
visa_types.purpose: Visa-free entry for holders of diplomatic or official passports under bilateral agreements between Belize and various countries.
visa_types.notes: Diplomatic and Official Passport holders are generally admitted for up to 30 days. Specific bilateral agreements extend exemptions to service and official passports of Hungary (90 days), Pakistan (90 days), Ecuador, Dominican Republic, and Venezuela (diplomatic/official only, ordinary passports require visas). Benelux states (Belgium, Netherlands, Luxembourg) diplomatic passport holders exempt under May 2025 agreement. Benin holders of diplomatic, official, service, and ordinary passports exempt under November 2024 agreement.

outbound_official.note: no official Belize government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data. Searched mfa.gov.bz (Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Foreign Trade) and immigration.gov.bz (Department of Immigration and Nationality) - both sites focus exclusively on inbound immigration to Belize and do not publish a list of countries where Belize ordinary-passport citizens may travel visa-free, visa-on-arrival, via e-visa, or ETA.
